segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a modern, well-lit kitchen where Ed O’Neill, a man in a dark sweater and collared shirt, walks in smiling. Sofía Vergara, a pregnant woman in a blue wrap dress with long wavy hair, large hoop earrings, and a silver bracelet, gestures animatedly while speaking to him in a distinct accent, asking if he's seen the grapes. Ed O'Neill, with a deeper voice, admits he ate them, prompting Sofía to question why, explaining they were for the New Year's tradition of eating 12 grapes for 12 wishes. Ed O'Neill expresses skepticism about the tradition's effectiveness. Sofía Vergara passionately defends it, recounting her journey from a single mother in a slum to driving a new car to Palm Springs with her rich husband, concluding she's eating the grapes. The scene then cuts to a close-up of Ed O’Neill speaking directly to the camera in an interview setting, identified by on-screen text, where he describes New Year's Eve plans to take Phil, Claire, Mitch, and Cam to an amazing hotel in Palm Springs, detailing a package including dinner, dancing, and a midnight toast. The video then shifts to a warm, domestic dining area, featuring red curtains, a patterned rug, and a side table with a lamp. Here, a man in a light blue long-sleeve shirt sits at a table with food, gesturing as he speaks to a woman with curly blonde hair in a light blue button-up shirt, who leans forward listening intently. The man, with a distinct male voice, discusses watching an 'incredible telekinesis video' and attempting to make a piece of bacon levitate. The woman, with a distinct female voice, questions if he shouldn't be working. He then asks if she's implying it won't happen, seeking a 'more colorful way of saying that,' to which she replies he wants her to say it'll happen 'when pigs fly.' He concludes, 'That counts!'
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens in a modern, well-lit kitchen where a man in a dark sweater and collared shirt walks in, smiling, as a pregnant woman in a blue wrap dress stands near the counter. She gestures animatedly while speaking to him, her long wavy hair flowing, and she wears large hoop earrings and a silver bracelet. The man listens, then turns to face her, engaging in conversation. The scene cuts to a close-up of the man speaking directly to the camera in an interview setting, with text overlays identifying him as 'Ed O’Neill' and 'Sofía Vergara' appearing on screen. The scene shifts again to a dining area where a man in a light blue long-sleeve shirt sits at a table with a plate of food, gesturing with his hands as he speaks to a woman with curly blonde hair wearing a light blue button-up shirt. She leans forward, listening intently, and the room features red curtains, a patterned rug, and a side table with a lamp, creating a warm, domestic atmosphere.

Later, in an interview setting, he describes New Year's Eve plans for a trip to Palm Springs, including dinner, dancing, and a midnight toast.

She speaks with a distinct accent, animatedly discussing a New Year's grape-eating tradition, passionately defending its significance by sharing her personal journey, and reiterating her intention to eat the grapes.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a warmly lit kitchen where a Man in a blue long-sleeve shirt, seated at a dining table, gestures animatedly, exclaiming 'What!' and 'Oh my god!' to a Woman with curly blonde hair, who leans forward intently. She greets, 'Good morning, Earthmates,' to which the man responds, 'Morning, Farah.' The woman then complains, 'Oh, my lower back is killing me. Has anyone seen my healing crystal?' and the man sarcastically replies, 'That may be the only thing of yours we haven't seen.' A female voice, belonging to the Woman with dark hair and glasses, then provides a voiceover narration, stating, 'Dylan's mom, Farah, came to see the babies, and then she found out that her polyamorous quad was downgraded to a triad. Now we're stuck with her.' Back in the kitchen, the man reacts to this, asking, 'Polyamory, can you imagine?' The voiceover concludes with a firm 'She needs to go.' The scene transitions with upbeat, orchestral, dramatic background music, typical of a TV show intro, to a promotional graphic for 'Modern Family' stickers, showcasing seven original designs with humorous quotes. The final segment shifts to a cluttered pantry where the Woman with dark hair and glasses, holding a black object, speaks to a Young boy in a plaid shirt. She says, 'I'm sorry you're having a bad day, but I thought you should know, I invited Danielle.' The boy, with a high-pitched, whiny voice, complains, 'Danielle? Oh, she's so annoying. Everything she says sounds like a question, but it's not?' The woman defends, 'Yeah, but she's in her like thousandth month of pregnancy,' prompting the boy to exclaim, 'Oh my god, that means I won't be the grossest preggo here!'

The scene transitions to a different setting where the same man and woman are now seated on a couch, appearing in a more relaxed, interview-style setup, with the woman in an orange ruffled top and the man in a blue button-up shirt, both engaging in conversation. The video then cuts to a promotional graphic for 'Modern Family' stickers, showcasing seven original designs with humorous quotes and character illustrations, displayed on various items like a laptop, mug, and phone case, with text emphasizing 'JUST FOR LIMITED TIME' and 'STICK THEM ANYWHERE'. The pantry shelves are stocked with cleaning supplies and household items, and the woman gestures expressively as she talks, with the boy listening attentively.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a cluttered pantry where a young boy in a plaid shirt stands beside a woman with dark hair and glasses, wearing a black patterned blouse. She gestures while speaking to him, and her voice is heard saying "miss you!" followed by a sigh. The scene shifts to a kitchen where a man in a plaid shirt wipes a counter with a cloth, while another man in a checkered shirt stands nearby. One of these men expresses confusion and concern, asking "What spice is in here? Is that dill? Where the hell is dill in here? It's a catastrophe," followed by sounds of a door opening and closing. The setting then transitions to a festive environment with balloons and party decorations, where a man in a blue sweater holds a blue cup and a phone, conversing with a woman with long dark hair holding a pink cup. The man thanks her for inviting him, but expresses feeling like he doesn't belong, while the woman confirms he is "the baby's father." Later, the man discusses losing track of the "Black Widow" and asks "Are you scared?", to which a woman (possibly the same one or a new voice) responds with "What?!" and asserts, "She should be scared of you. If she's smart, that young lady will run and keep running." The man in the blue sweater walks away, and the scene cuts to a man in a striped shirt eating a sandwich while a young girl in a pink jacket stands beside him. The final scene shows a woman with blonde hair in a purple top adjusting the hair of a young girl in a dark hoodie in a dimly lit, warm, and intimate atmosphere. The woman's voice is heard explaining her plan to "repurpose some of this hair to cover the bald spot."

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a dimly lit room where a woman with blonde hair in a purple top adjusts the hair of a young girl in a dark hoodie, confidently declaring "it's gonna look amazing. Ta-da!" The girl echoes "Ta-da!" before the woman humorously adds, "I have a huge gut in a comb over. I look like a divorced dentist." A door then opens to a brightly lit kitchen, revealing a baby shower setting with decorations and gifts. A pregnant Sofía Vergara, with long wavy brown hair, wearing a blue wrap dress and large hoop earrings, cradles her belly as she enters, greeted by a surprised woman with dark hair and glasses in a black patterned blouse who exclaims, "Oh, Danielle! You look so good!" Sofía (addressed as Danielle) attributes her glow to "the magic of pregnancy, #blessed." The woman with glasses then asks, "What the hell? Alex said it was okay if I came early?" Another voice then addresses Sofía as "Hailey," saying, "I'm so happy for you!" A child's voice urgently asks, "I need Dylan, where is Dylan? Where's Dylan?" followed by a young boy in a white vest and bow tie dramatically declaring, "Hailey's not gonna find Dylan. He's gone, and it's everybody's fault!" The scene shifts to a festive outdoor night event with string lights. A male voice attempts to lighten the mood, asking, "What is up with all the funeral faces? Come on people, this is supposed to be a baby shower!" with another voice adding, "Shower! Nobody wants it." A man in a dark suit with a purple tie is heard explaining, "I can't fit more than 30 people on the boat," to which a man in a pilot’s uniform, laughing and gesturing animatedly, humorously interjects, "First of all, that lady is my son." The man in the suit clarifies, "He wasn't talking about me," but the pilot continues, "Talking about her. Oh, her. Oh, it's her now." The man in the suit then protectively concludes, "Well, her happens to be my wife. You got a problem with her, you got a problem with me."
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a woman with blonde hair wearing a purple top, adjusting the hair of a young girl in a dark hoodie in a dimly lit room, suggesting a moment of preparation or care. The scene quickly shifts to a brightly lit kitchen where a pregnant woman with long, wavy brown hair, wearing a blue wrap dress and large hoop earrings, is greeted warmly by a woman with dark hair and glasses in a black patterned blouse. The pregnant woman, identified as Sofía Vergara, cradles her belly as she enters, and the woman with dark hair and glasses appears surprised or emotional, covering her mouth. The setting is a baby shower, evident from the decorations, gifts, and a table with a cake and snacks. As the camera pans, a young boy in a white vest and bow tie stands near the table, looking around curiously. The scene then cuts to a festive outdoor night event, where a man in a pilot’s uniform with epaulets is seen laughing and gesturing animatedly while conversing with others. He is surrounded by guests in formal attire, including a man in a dark suit with a purple tie who appears to be speaking earnestly. The atmosphere is lively, with string lights and a purple-lit backdrop, suggesting a celebratory gathering. The pilot continues to engage with the group, smiling and gesturing, indicating a joyful interaction.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens at a lively outdoor night event, illuminated by string lights, where a man with short gray hair, wearing a dark suit and purple tie (addressed as 'Jay'), animatedly speaks to a woman with long dark hair (addressed as 'Gloria'). Jay's voice is initially calm as he says "Calm down," but then expresses frustration, stating he "didn't have a problem before, but now I'm starting to." Gloria responds with expressive hand movements, her distinct female voice pleading, "No, Jay, I know you think this is what I want, but it's not," and later, "I was just being stupid today." An assertive male voice interjects, warning Jay his "problem's about to get a lot bigger," and later tells him to "walk away, grandpa," before declaring, "Okay, that's it. No boat. Everybody off my dock or I'm calling the cops." Gloria shouts, "Nobody! Go get him, grandpa!" as the argument escalates, while a man in a pilot’s uniform laughs and gestures in the background. The scene abruptly cuts to a bright daytime suburban street, where the same man, Jay, now in a black leather jacket and helmet, rides a red motorcycle toward a couple by their driveway. The motorcycle's engine revs loudly as he approaches. A man in a dark sweater, speaking with a calm male voice, says "Oh, okay," and expresses concern, "I don't want the bike all scratched up." The woman with blonde hair, speaking with a concerned female voice, advises, "Now be careful, I don't want you to crash," and "don't be nervous." Jay, with a confident and playful male voice, dismisses her concern, boasting, "I'm not nervous. I've had bigger hogs than this between my legs." He then revs the engine and rides away down the tree-lined street, leaving the surprised and concerned couple watching him go. As he departs, Jay's internal monologue reveals a reflective side, stating, "He really ought to run things through his head first," and "I know, I looked super cool on the outside. But on the inside..."
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens at a lively outdoor night event, possibly a party or celebration, with string lights and a festive atmosphere. A man in a dark suit with a purple tie (Man in dark suit with purple tie) is seen animatedly speaking to a woman with long dark hair (Woman with long dark hair), gesturing with his hands as if explaining or arguing something. The woman responds with expressive hand movements, appearing engaged and possibly surprised or defensive. The camera briefly captures a man in a pilot’s uniform (Man in pilot’s uniform) laughing and gesturing in the background, adding to the social energy. The scene then cuts to a daytime suburban street where a man in a black leather jacket and helmet (new_entity: Man on motorcycle) rides a motorcycle toward a couple standing by their driveway. The man on the motorcycle, who appears to be the same character from the earlier scene (Man in dark suit with purple tie) but now in casual biker attire, stops and interacts with the couple — a man in a dark sweater (Man in dark sweater) and a woman in a purple shirt (Woman with blonde hair). The couple looks surprised and slightly concerned as the biker speaks to them. The biker then revs the engine and rides away down the tree-lined street, leaving the couple standing in place, watching him go. The lighting shifts from the warm, artificial glow of the night party to the bright, natural daylight of the suburban neighborhood, marking a clear transition in time and setting.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a man in a black and white checkered shirt, seated on a couch in a brightly lit living room, speaking directly to the camera in a reflective tone about being 'terrified' and remembering 'Fonzie' putting together a motorcycle blind, which distracted him from his fear. The scene quickly transitions to an outdoor setting where the same man, now wearing a black leather jacket and helmet, confidently rides a red motorcycle with dual headlights along a dusty, sunlit dirt road, kicking up dust. His narration continues, describing the exhilaration of heading 'up the canyon,' leaving the city behind, and feeling 'one' with the bike, before the sounds of a crash, his panicked exclamations of 'Oh no! No! Oh, you're heavy! No!', and animal growls indicate an accident, followed by his final 'Uh oh.' The video then cuts to a suburban street where a man in a dark suit with a purple tie, holding a rolled-up poster and a red box, walks with a man in a dark polo shirt towards an ivy-covered house. During this, a young boy's voice is heard asking, 'Hello ma'am, do you love Christmas?', to which a woman's voice replies, 'Actually, I'm Jewish.' The boy then suggests, 'Well then, you must appreciate a good value,' prompting a male voice to sigh, 'Oh jeez.' The scene then shifts to a baby’s nursery with pink walls, where a woman in a black t-shirt and glasses is folding a pink blanket near a crib, while a soft, melodic male voice sings a lullaby about the moon shining and finally finding its place. The final shot shows a woman in a purple shirt in a kitchen, pouring liquid from a yellow bottle into a pot, with credits rolling over the scene. Throughout, on-screen text includes '@modernfamilyshow' and 'STICKER PACK LINK IN BIO' with a graphic of '7 ORIGINAL STICKERS'.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a man in a light blue shirt, seen from behind, adjusting his appearance in a bathroom mirror with patterned tiles as credits roll. The scene then cuts to a man in a dark shirt smiling while shaving with a magnifying mirror, followed by a red-haired man with glasses laughing as he applies shaving cream, all while a bluesy, somewhat off-key acapella song is sung, with lyrics like "baby, baby, I just wanna do you" and healing "from the inside." A calm male voice then states, "No midlife crisis, I just think you're never too old to learn." The setting abruptly shifts to a dimly lit auditorium or classroom where a young boy in a black suit and bow tie sits attentively, arms crossed, beside a man in a gray blazer. During this, a young female voice is heard saying, "Just stay away from my mom. She is a thing for losers." The camera pans across an audience of adults and teens, some holding programs, as sounds of heavy straining and dialogue about contractions getting closer together, with a female voice saying, "Dad is next, I can hold it for a few minutes, okay? I don't want to screw this up for him," create a tense, dramatic interlude. This is abruptly followed by a loud "WOO!" and enthusiastic applause and cheering from the audience. The focus returns to the boy and the man, who exchange glances, indicating engagement with the event. A female voice then asks, "Yikes. It's all that. What are you gonna dazzle us with?" A male voice responds, "For Elise?" and another female voice queries, "In which key?" The final shot shows the boy and man seated among others on a stage or front row, with a black grand piano visible to the side, reinforcing the performance context under cool, blue-toned lighting, creating a formal, slightly tense atmosphere.

The camera then cuts to another man in a dark shirt, also in a bathroom, using a magnifying mirror and a razor, smiling as he shaves. The scene shifts to a third man with red hair and glasses, who is also shaving in front of a mirror, laughing as he applies shaving cream. The setting then changes to a dimly lit auditorium or classroom where a young boy in a black suit with a bow tie sits beside a man in a gray blazer, both looking attentively forward. The camera pans across an audience of adults and teens, some holding programs, suggesting a performance or presentation is underway. The focus returns to the boy and the man, who exchange glances and subtle reactions, indicating engagement with the event. The final shot shows the boy and the man seated among others on a stage or front row, with a piano visible to the side, reinforcing the performance context. The lighting is cool and blue-toned, creating a formal, slightly tense atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a dimly lit auditorium with red curtains, where a woman in a beige cardigan and glasses, acting as a host, introduces the next performer, "the only student of mine who shaves," Philip Dunphy, to applause. The camera pans to the audience, showing a young boy in a black suit with a bow tie seated attentively beside a man in a gray blazer. This man, Philip Dunphy, then walks to the grand piano, sits down, and begins to play a brief, focused piece. After his performance, he stands, bows, and addresses the audience, stating that when he turned 50, he vowed to do things to take him out of his comfort zone and keep him on his toes, thanking his family for being his "biggest cheerleaders," which elicits laughter and cheers. His speech is abruptly interrupted by urgent off-screen voices announcing "Haley's going into labor!" and an apology from Philip, "Oh my god, I'm so sorry everyone," followed by a sarcastic "Convenient" from another off-screen voice. Despite the commotion, Philip Dunphy resumes his performance, playing a complex classical piece on the piano. He finishes, stands, bows, and walks off stage to loud applause and encouraging shouts of "Great work, honey!" He then tells someone off-screen to "Tell your mom I say hi." The scene transitions to a brightly lit home kitchen where upbeat, lighthearted background music begins. A pregnant Sofía Vergara, in a blue floral dress, enters smiling and cradling her belly, as other family members, including a man in a dark sweater and a young boy in a blue t-shirt, gather around her, suggesting a baby shower or family celebration. An off-screen woman's voice comments, "Oh gross, they're at it again," hinting at a recurring family dynamic.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens in a dimly lit auditorium with red curtains, where a piano recital is taking place. A woman in a beige cardigan and glasses, likely a teacher or host, stands on stage gesturing as she speaks to the audience. The camera pans to show the audience, including a young boy in a black suit with a bow tie seated beside a man in a gray blazer, both observing attentively. The man in the gray blazer then walks to the grand piano, sits down, and begins to play with focus. After a brief performance, he stands, bows, and walks off stage, receiving applause from the audience. The scene transitions to a brightly lit home kitchen where a pregnant woman in a blue floral dress, Sofía Vergara, enters smiling, cradling her belly, as other family members, including a man in a dark sweater and a young boy in a blue t-shirt, gather around her, suggesting a baby shower or family celebration. The atmosphere shifts from formal and quiet to warm and joyful.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a modern, well-lit kitchen where a man with a beard in a light gray button-up shirt stands behind a woman with blonde hair in a black top, placing his hand on her shoulder as she gestures with her hand. The man calmly remarks, "If you don't like it, don't look," and adds, "She looks great, doesn't she?" The woman responds, "Yeah. Not really gaining weight the way I would have expected," to which the man replies, "I finally understand why people say pregnant women glow," and the woman quips, "Feel like I had three children." The scene transitions to a living room where a man, identified as Phil, in a light blue long-sleeve shirt sits on a beige couch with colorful striped pillows, clasping his hands and smiling warmly at the camera, speaking in a confessional style about his birthday, stating, "Key to a good birthday? Low expectations," and lamenting, "Thought I was getting an iPad... Thought I was getting a surprise party at the batting cages. Sorry, Big Phil." He concludes, "Guess when it comes right down to it, we're all just Jeff Sweeny taking a fastball to the plums." The setting then shifts to a hallway filled with balloons, accompanied by a crashing sound. A young girl in a plaid shirt, walking down the stairs beside a man in a dark zip-up hoodie, informs her "Dad" (Phil, the man from the couch), "Hey, um, Dad, your cake fell off the counter, but Uncle Cam's trying to fix it." Phil dismissively replies, "He won't," and when the girl adds, "And the pizza's never showed up," he again states, "And they won't." A "boing" sound is heard as Phil exclaims, "Hey, balloons!" The man in the dark zip-up hoodie, smiling, is joined by a woman with long dark hair in a black top and another man in a dark shirt carrying a large wrapped gift, indicating a celebratory occasion. The man in the hoodie calmly says, "Fine, I guess we'll never know." Phil then asks, "Hey, Manny, give me the chess set," to which a young boy in an orange shirt excitedly responds, "Oh, it's a chess set!" Phil tells him, "Yeah, you'll get it later. We get something to celebrate right now, don't we?" as he wishes, "Happy birthday, Phil!" The man in the hoodie echoes, "Happy, happy..." and the woman with long dark hair adds, "Is it..." The atmosphere is cheerful and familial, with a red-themed promotional sticker graphic for '7 ORIGINAL STICKERS' from 'Modern Family' appearing in the top left corner, featuring character illustrations and humorous quotes, with text 'STICKER PACK LINK IN BIO'.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a cozy, well-lit living room, decorated with balloons and flowers for a celebration. A man in a dark zip-up hoodie descends the stairs with a warm smile, his voice heard saying, "Claire's baked the brie, I smell," followed by a chuckle, as he's greeted by a young boy in an orange shirt. Another voice responds, "No." The man then sits on a beige couch, opens 'The Art of War,' and begins reading with his feet up, his excited voice exclaiming, "Oh Cam, Cam, you'll never believe what happened." In the background, a man in a plaid shirt and a woman holding a baby descend the stairs, as a voice shouts, "Happy Birthday, Phil!" and another responds, "Sure!" The man in the dark zip-up hoodie continues, "You'll never believe what happened. I got in trouble for fighting." Other voices add, "And we saved a marriage," "Well, this is the best day ever," "It really is," and "Ain't it though?" The scene shifts to a chess game between Ed O’Neill and a woman with long dark hair, with the young boy watching intently. The woman's voice is heard saying, "Look at this, huh?" followed by triumphant laughter after she makes a move. Ed O’Neill reacts thoughtfully, then leans back, his calm voice responding, "Easy. You can't sing 'We are the Champions' without your queen," appearing to concede. The woman laughs triumphantly, then exclaims in frustration, "Damn it! Listen, I lose and I burn this house down!" while Ed O’Neill stands up smiling and the boy claps. She continues, "Ah, but look at this..." Ed O’Neill, with a resigned tone, says, "Honey, what are we doing? This is ridiculous," elaborating, "One of us is going to win the game, the other's going to feel lousy and we both lose." A loud crashing sound abruptly punctuates the scene, after which Ed O’Neill sarcastically remarks, "Yeah, just leave my birthday present on the floor," then affectionately adds, "That's my girl," and concludes, "She had me. There was no way I could win. I knew it." The scene then cuts abruptly to a young girl in a denim vest sitting in a car, holding a blue toy and looking out at a green, rolling landscape, her voice heard asking, "What you got? Hurricane Harriet."

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens inside a moving train, where a young girl in a denim vest over a red shirt and red patterned pants excitedly shows a blue folder labeled 'Happy' to a woman in a black leather jacket. The girl, speaking in a high-pitched, excited voice, describes 'Harriet' as a "ditsy girl who causes problems for everyone but never realizes it," prompting the woman in the leather jacket to respond that it "doesn't sound very realistic." A jolt and the sounds of the moving train punctuate their conversation. The scene then shifts to a woman in a dark blue dress with long wavy hair confidently walking down the train aisle, passing a train door marked 'COACH'. She interacts with a male train attendant in a vest and tie, asking in a slightly frantic adult voice about the bathroom. The attendant, in a firm adult voice, asks for a "First class ticket, please." The woman explains her ticket is in her seat, and the annoyed attendant tells her to "just go back where you came from," which she defiantly repeats, adding, "I see how it is." As she continues through the car, passing another woman in a brown top, she suddenly yells, "They have me!" The camera cuts back to a close-up of the young girl and the woman in the leather jacket, who are now holding hands and smiling warmly. The girl, still in her excited, high-pitched voice, continues her story, describing Harriet sending people "right into a bear cave," and the woman in the leather jacket exclaims, "What an idiot!" On-screen text displays producer credits throughout the train scenes. The setting then transitions from the dimly lit train interior to a bright outdoor shot of a man in a black leather jacket looking up at the sun, his face illuminated, while a distressed, echoing male voice asks, "Can anyone hear me? Please help me!"
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens inside a moving train, where a young girl in a denim vest over a red shirt and red patterned pants is seated next to a woman in a black leather jacket. The girl excitedly shows the woman a blue folder labeled 'Happy' with a cartoon character, and they share a moment of laughter and connection. The scene shifts to a woman in a dark blue dress with long wavy hair walking down the train aisle, smiling and interacting with a male train attendant in a vest and tie, and another woman in a brown top. The woman in the blue dress appears cheerful and confident as she moves through the car, passing by passengers. The camera then cuts to a close-up of the young girl and the woman in the leather jacket, who are now holding hands and smiling warmly at each other. The scene transitions to a bright outdoor shot of a man in a black leather jacket looking up at the sun, his face illuminated by the light, suggesting a moment of reflection or awe. Throughout the segment, on-screen text displays credits for executive and co-executive producers, including 'jeffrey kichman', 'stephen lloyd', 'jeff morton', 'valichandrasekaran', 'chuck tatham', and 'andy gordon'. The setting shifts from the dimly lit interior of the train to the bright, open sky, marking a transition in mood and environment.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a modern, well-lit kitchen where a group is gathered around a white island. A man in a blue and black striped polo shirt (Man in striped shirt) comments, "But it seems kinda silly now what we're even fighting about, huh?" A young boy in a black shirt, looking slightly uncomfortable, explains, "I made fun of his accent." Sofía Vergara, in a black sequined dress, exclaims with her distinct accent, "What accent!" The boy continues, "I made fun of him for having the same thing for lunch every day," which is followed by laughter. He adds, "I made fun of him because his mom used to dig coal." The Man in striped shirt asks, "What?" and the boy clarifies, "He said you're a coal digger." The man quickly tries to move on, "Okay, I think we can move on." Sofía Vergara, pointing and speaking with enthusiasm, challenges, "Who said I was a coal digger?" The boy responds, "That's what my mom told me." A woman with dark hair and glasses, pouring a drink from a bottle, asks, "What's a coal digger?" A man in a light blue shirt, conversing with a woman with blonde hair at the sink, corrects, "Sweetheart, you heard it wrong. It's gold digger." The woman with blonde hair, engaged in conversation, says, "I'm gonna have to call you back," and then denies, "Well, I really do not think that I remember ever saying that." The young boy in black shirt insists, "Well, you said in the car, you said at Christmas." Another young boy in a green shirt, holding a red football, chimes in, "You said in the Mexican..." before being cut off. The woman with blonde hair, addressing Sofía Vergara as "Gloria," sarcastically retorts, "Okay, mister, leaves his sweatshirts at school every day, suddenly remembers everything. Thank you." Sofía Vergara, with her accent, then asks, "Honey, what's all in my head, huh?" The woman with blonde hair begins to respond, "Oh, listen, Gloria, it it was like..." before the scene transitions. The video concludes with a red promotional graphic advertising '7 ORIGINAL STICKERS' from 'Modern Family', featuring character illustrations and humorous quotes, displayed on a laptop, mug, and phone case, all set to upbeat, orchestral, and dramatic music.

audio_updated_entities.Man in light blue shirt: A man with dark hair, wearing a light blue short-sleeve shirt, standing beside the woman with blonde hair, facing her during their conversation, and clarifying that the term is "gold digger," not "coal digger."
audio_updated_entities.Young boy in black shirt: A young boy with short dark hair, wearing a black short-sleeve shirt, standing beside the man in the striped shirt, appearing slightly uncomfortable or reserved, who speaks about making fun of someone's accent, their lunch habits, and their mom being a "coal digger," and later clarifies that his mom told him this, and recalls someone saying things "in the car" and "at Christmas."
audio_updated_entities.Young boy in green shirt: A young boy with curly brown hair, wearing a green t-shirt over a white long-sleeve shirt, holding a red football and standing beside the boy in black, who contributes to the conversation by saying "You said in the Mexican..." before being interrupted.

entity_library.Ed O’Neill: A man with short gray hair, wearing a dark sweater over a collared shirt, seated at a chess table, playing against a woman with long dark hair. He displays thoughtful and expressive reactions during the game, his calm voice heard responding, 'Easy. You can't sing 'We are the Champions' without your queen,' as he appears to concede. He later stands up smiling, and with a resigned tone, says, 'Honey, what are we doing? This is ridiculous,' elaborating, 'One of us is going to win the game, the other's going to feel lousy and we both lose.' After a crashing sound, he sarcastically remarks, 'Yeah, just leave my birthday present on the floor,' then affectionately adds, 'That's my girl,' and concludes, 'She had me. There was no way I could win. I knew it.'
entity_library.Sofía Vergara: A woman with long, wavy brown hair, wearing a black dress with sequined shoulders, identified as Gloria, standing near the kitchen island, smiling, pointing, and speaking with a distinct accent, exclaiming "What accent!", questioning "Who said I was a coal digger?", and asking "Honey, what's all in my head, huh?"
entity_library.Man in blue long-sleeve shirt: A man with dark hair, wearing a light blue long-sleeve shirt, initially seated at a dining table gesturing animatedly, exclaiming 'What!' and 'Oh my god!', addressing the woman as 'Farah,' making a sarcastic comment about her 'healing crystal,' and later reacting to a narration about polyamory with 'Polyamory, can you imagine?'. He is later seen in a blue button-up shirt seated on a couch in a relaxed interview setting.
entity_library.Woman with curly blonde hair: A woman with curly blonde hair, initially wearing a light blue button-up shirt, leaning forward at a dining table, greeting 'Good morning, Earthmates,' and complaining about her 'lower back' while asking about a 'healing crystal.' She is later seen in an orange ruffled top, seated on a couch, engaging in conversation.
entity_library.Woman with dark hair and glasses: A woman with dark hair and glasses, wearing a purple shirt, pouring a drink from a bottle at the kitchen counter, and asking "What's a coal digger?"
entity_library.Young boy in plaid shirt: A young boy with light brown hair, wearing a plaid shirt, standing beside the woman in the pantry, listening attentively. He has a high-pitched, whiny voice, complaining about 'Danielle' saying 'Oh, she's so annoying. Everything she says sounds like a question, but it's not?' and later exclaiming 'Oh my god, that means I won't be the grossest preggo here!'
entity_library.Promotional sticker graphic: A red-themed graphic advertising '7 ORIGINAL STICKERS' from 'Modern Family', featuring character illustrations and humorous quotes, displayed on various products like a laptop, mug, and phone case, with text emphasizing limited-time availability. This is accompanied by upbeat, orchestral, dramatic background music.
entity_library.Man in plaid shirt: A man with dark hair, wearing a plaid shirt, descending the stairs while holding a baby, contributing to the celebratory atmosphere.
entity_library.Man in checkered shirt: A man with dark hair, wearing a black and white checkered shirt, seated on a couch, speaking reflectively about overcoming fear, remembering 'Fonzie,' and getting distracted. His voice is later heard narrating an exhilarating motorcycle ride and then exclaiming in panic during a crash.
entity_library.Man in blue sweater: A man with short red hair, wearing a blue sweater over a collared shirt, holding a blue cup and a phone, conversing with a woman in a festive room decorated with balloons, expressing gratitude for the invitation but also a feeling of not belonging, and later discussing the 'Black Widow' and asking 'Are you scared?'.
entity_library.Woman with long dark hair: A woman with long dark hair, wearing a black top, playing chess with Ed O’Neill. She shows animated expressions and celebrates after making a winning move, her voice heard saying, 'Look at this, huh?' followed by triumphant laughter. She later exclaims in frustration, 'Damn it! Listen, I lose and I burn this house down!' and continues, 'Ah, but look at this...'
entity_library.Man in striped shirt: A man with short dark hair, wearing a blue and black striped polo shirt, placing his hand on a young boy's shoulder and gesturing animatedly while speaking lines like "But it seems kinda silly now what we're even fighting about, huh?", "What?", and "Okay, I think we can move on."

entity_library.Young boy in white vest and bow tie: A young boy with light brown hair, wearing a white vest and bow tie, standing near a table at the baby shower, looking around curiously. He dramatically declares, 'Hailey's not gonna find Dylan. He's gone, and it's everybody's fault!'.

entity_library.Man in gray blazer: A man with dark hair, wearing a gray blazer over a light blue shirt, initially seated in the auditorium, then identified as Philip Dunphy. He plays the grand piano, speaks about his personal journey of turning 50 and thanking his family, apologizes for an interruption, and later speaks to someone off-screen, all with a clear male voice.

entity_library.Man in light blue long-sleeve shirt: A man with dark hair, wearing a light blue long-sleeve shirt, identified as 'Dad' and 'Phil', seated on a beige couch with colorful striped pillows, clasping his hands and smiling warmly while speaking directly to the camera about his birthday expectations, and later heard reacting dismissively to birthday mishaps and asking for a chess set.
entity_library.Man in dark zip-up hoodie: A man with short dark hair, wearing a dark zip-up hoodie over a light blue collared shirt, walking down a staircase with a warm smile, his voice heard saying, 'Claire's baked the brie, I smell,' followed by a chuckle. He later sits on a couch reading a book titled 'The Art of War' with his feet up, excitedly exclaiming, 'Oh Cam, Cam, you'll never believe what happened,' and continuing, 'You'll never believe what happened. I got in trouble for fighting.'

entity_library.Young girl in denim vest: A young girl with dark hair, wearing a denim vest over a red shirt and red patterned pants, seated in a train, holding a blue folder labeled 'Happy' with a cartoon character. She speaks in a high-pitched, excited voice, describing 'Harriet' as a 'ditsy girl who causes problems' and later as someone who sends people 'right into a bear cave'.
entity_library.Woman in black leather jacket: A woman with medium-length brown hair, wearing a black leather jacket over a dark top, seated in a train, engaging with the young girl. She responds to the girl's story with a calm adult voice, stating something 'doesn't sound very realistic,' and later exclaims, 'What an idiot!'

She speaks in an adult, slightly frantic voice, asking about the bathroom, then defiantly repeats the attendant's instruction, and later yells, 'They have me!'
entity_library.Male train attendant: A man with short dark hair, wearing a white shirt, dark vest, and tie, with a name tag and ID badge, standing in the train aisle. He speaks in a firm, adult voice, asking for a 'First class ticket, please,' and later, sounding annoyed, tells the woman to 'just go back where you came from.'
